summary: SDK silently relays consumer telemetry to maintainer-controlled Feishu webhooks by default
details: When a consumer initializes the ScanOrder or VenueBooking solution from this SDK without supplying their own loggerConfig/scanOrderLoggerConfig, the package falls back to hardcoded Feishu (Lark) webhook URLs owned by the maintainer (https://open.feishu.cn/open-apis/bot/v2/hook/216b3fe6-... and.../015b7c2a-..., among four total). The Feishu provider in dist/solution/ScanOrder/index.js (lines 545-546) unconditionally POSTs every method-level log entry — including cacheId, method arguments, error stacks, and the consumer browser's window.location.host — to those webhooks via fetch. The README does not disclose this telemetry channel, and there is no opt-out short of explicitly overriding loggerConfig at registration time. Any application that integrates this SDK and exercises the ScanOrder/VenueBooking flows will leak its operational data (scanned codes, customer-context arguments, host identifiers, error payloads) to a maintainer-controlled chat. Compounding the trust problem, package.json carries placeholder publisher metadata (author 'Your Name', repository and homepage under 'github.com/username/pisell-os'), so the destination is not even tied to an identifiable maintainer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
